intro:
Spin the wheel is a fun, interactive random decision-making tool that brings excitement and fairness to any choice. Whether you are picking a restaurant for dinner, selecting a random winner for a giveaway, assigning tasks to team members, or making any decision that requires random selection, a spinning wheel delivers instant, visually engaging results.

Our free spin the wheel tool lets you customize the wheel with your own options, set different weightings if desired, and spin away. The animated wheel adds an element of suspense and fun that simple random number generators lack. It is perfect for classrooms, parties, business meetings, and family decisions.

Teachers use spinning wheels to randomly call on students or assign groups. Businesses use them for prize draws and team-building activities. Families use them to settle debates about what to watch, where to eat, or who does the dishes. The possibilities are endless when you have a customizable, interactive random picker.
howItWorks: Add your options to the wheel by typing each choice and clicking Add. You can add as many segments as you like and remove any you do not want. Click Spin to set the wheel in motion. The wheel spins with realistic physics and slows to a stop on a random selection. The winning segment is highlighted and announced clearly.
interpretationGuide: The result shown after spinning is a fair random selection from all segments on the wheel. Each segment has an equal chance of winning unless you assign different weights. If certain options should appear more frequently, add them multiple times to increase their probability of selection.

faqs.question: How random is the spin?
faqs.answer: The spin uses a cryptographically random seed to determine the stopping position, making each spin statistically independent and fair. Every segment has an equal probability of landing.

faqs.question: Can I save my wheel for later?
faqs.answer: Many implementations save your wheel options in browser local storage so they persist when you return. You can also bookmark the page after customizing if the URL encodes your options.

faqs.question: How many options can I add?
faqs.answer: You can typically add up to 100 options on the wheel. With very large numbers of segments, individual segments become thin, so 20-30 options usually provide the best visual experience.

faqs.question: Can I weight certain options to appear more?
faqs.answer: Yes, by adding the same option multiple times, you increase its relative probability. For example, adding Option A twice and Option B once gives Option A a 67% chance of being selected.

faqs.question: Is this good for classroom use?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Teachers use spinning wheels to randomly select students to answer questions, assign groups, pick reading topics, and make classroom decisions feel fair and exciting for students.

faqs.question: Can I use this for giveaways?
faqs.answer: Yes. Add participant names to the wheel and spin to randomly select a winner. It is transparent and visually engaging for live giveaways on streams, events, or social media.

faqs.question: Can I remove the winning option after each spin?
faqs.answer: Yes, many spinning wheels have a Remove after spin option that eliminates the winner from subsequent spins, useful when selecting multiple unique winners in sequence.

faqs.question: Does the wheel work on mobile?
faqs.answer: Yes, the spinning wheel is fully responsive and touch-friendly. You can tap the wheel to spin it on smartphones and tablets.

faqs.question: What are good use cases for a spinning wheel?
faqs.answer: Common uses include restaurant selection, prize draws, task assignment, game decisions, classroom participation, choosing movies or games, settling friendly debates, and any situation needing fair random selection.

faqs.question: Can I spin the wheel multiple times?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can spin as many times as you like. Each spin is independent and random, making it perfect for elimination-style games where you need multiple selections.
